changes: OpenClaw shipped v2026.3.13 plus v2026.3.13-beta.1 on 2026-03-14, sustaining the project's near-daily release cadence.
SwarmClaw followed yesterday's positioning rewrite with a v1.0.8 release centered on learned skills, self-healing, gateway hardening, and operator UX fixes.
TinyClaw advanced to v0.0.13 and HiClaw to v1.0.5, keeping the orchestration / agent-team lane visibly active.
ZeroClaw published multiple same-day beta releases (v0.1.9-beta.141, .148, .150), reinforcing continued momentum in the ultra-lightweight Rust lane.
The tracked set stayed stable today: Hermes remains canonically tracked at NousResearch/hermes-agent, and no new project forced a watchlist or taxonomy change.
whyItMatters: OpenClaw is still setting the ecosystem tempo through release velocity, not just brand gravity.
SwarmClaw is backing its broader orchestration-runtime narrative with concrete shipped behavior rather than only a README rewrite.
Release activity across TinyClaw, HiClaw, and ZeroClaw shows momentum is distributed across multiple product lanes at once.
Because the taxonomy and monitored set did not change, today's signal is execution speed rather than a new strategic frame.
